articles.title: Orlando Health fleshes out Alabama footprint with another acquisition
articles.summary: The $10 billion nonprofit plans to acquire RMC Health System from the City of Anniston, building on 2024's big-ticket purchase of five Alabama hospitals from Tenet Healthcare.

articles.title: US adults still turn to providers for accurate health information even as AI chatbot use grows: Pew survey
articles.summary: Researchers surveyed more than 5,000 U.S. adults last October on where they seek out health information—and their views on how accurate results are.

articles.title: Shadow AI: Dos and don’ts for healthcare orgs
articles.summary: Unauthorized AI use is already happening inside healthcare organizations, and not always in ways leadership can see. A new survey from Wolters Kluwer suggests “shadow AI” is more common than many realize, raising questions about risk, workflow pressures and what responsible adoption should actually look like.

articles.title: Insurers have eliminated 11% of prior authorizations under reform pledge
articles.summary: Last summer, the insurance industry broadly agreed to reform a major healthcare pain point: prior authorization. Now, two of the industry's leading organizations are offering a look at progress toward those goals.

articles.title: Counsel Health expands primary AI care model to include lifestyle, chronic conditions
articles.summary: The artificial intelligence company will first focus on lifestyle conditions for the expansion, including chronic conditions throughout 2026.

articles.title: Waystar builds out AI solution to uncover lost provider revenue from payer 'take-backs'
articles.summary: Waystar developed an AI solution to help uncover lost provider revenue from payer payment 'take-backs.'

articles.title: Healthcare Dealmakers—Sutter Health, Allina Health's $26B combination, UHS' $835M Talkspace purchase and more
articles.summary: Providence is looking to sell its health plan, Cencora's $1.1 billion specialty care network deal and more healthcare merger and acquisition news from March.

articles.title: Ambience Healthcare launches Chart Chat, an EHR-integrated AI copilot for nurses
articles.summary: Nurses using the tool during its pilot at Cleveland Clinic say it is “helping them build a richer, more confident understanding” of patients, Ambience Healthcare Head of Product Tanha Kabir told Fierce Healthcare.

articles.title: Anthropic acquires stealth AI startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: reports
articles.summary: AI powerhouse Anthropic is continuing its push into the healthcare arena with the acquisition of previously stealth AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to reporting from The Information and Eric Newcomer.

articles.title: Prior authorization: Today’s innovators speak out
articles.summary: Across the session, speakers returned to one idea: the challenge is not prior authorization alone, but the fragmented, manual experience surrounding it. Here’s what they had to say.

articles.title: STAT+: Are AI scribes actually driving higher health care costs?
articles.summary: In this edition of STAT's AI Prognosis: A closer look at a recent analysis that implied AI scribes are driving up health care costs, and more.

articles.title: STAT+: Everyone agrees AI scribes are increasing health care costs. No one agrees what to do about it
articles.summary: Behind closed doors, health insurers and hospitals agree that AI scribes are bumping up health care costs. But, they don't agree on a solution.

articles.title: STAT+: States looking to regulate use of chatbots
articles.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: UnitedHealth Group's big bet on AI to transform its operations, venture funding trends, and more

articles.title: Insurers committed to cutting prior authorizations have eliminated 11% so far
articles.summary: Last summer, major payers committed to pare back onerous prior authorization policies, to the skepticism of providers. Now, AHIP and the Blue Cross Blue Shield Association are providing an update on insurers’ progress.

articles.title: Healthcare’s AI inflection point: The organizations that win will be the ones with the strongest data foundations
articles.summary: Healthcare doesn’t have an AI experimentation problem. It has an execution gap — and that gap is widening.

articles.title: An update on our mental health work
articles.summary: "Google Safety Updates: AI & Mental Health" is overlaid on an image of three Googlers.

articles.title: The next phase of enterprise AI
articles.summary: OpenAI outlines the next phase of enterprise AI, as adoption accelerates across industries with Frontier, ChatGPT Enterprise, Codex, and company-wide AI agents.

articles.title: Introducing the Child Safety Blueprint
articles.summary: Discover OpenAI’s Child Safety Blueprint—a roadmap for building AI responsibly with safeguards, age-appropriate design, and collaboration to protect and empower young people online.

articles.title: Industrial policy for the Intelligence Age
articles.summary: Explore our ambitious, people-first industrial policy ideas for the AI era—focused on expanding opportunity, sharing prosperity, and building resilient institutions as advanced intelligence evolves.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN
articles.summary: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.

articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs is launching its first model since Mark Zuckerberg spent billions overhauling the company's AI efforts. Called Muse Spark, the model now powers the Meta AI app and the Meta AI website in the US, per the company's announcement. In the coming weeks, Meta says, it will appear in WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, […]

articles.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
articles.summary: OpenAI is in a relatively precarious position. The company is and has been a funding behemoth - just over a week ago, it closed $122 billion in funding at a post-money valuation of $852 billion. It's potentially planning for an IPO later this year. ChatGPT's longtime lead in consumer-facing AI led it to name-brand status […]

articles.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
articles.summary: Unionized staff at ProPublica, one of the country's leading nonprofit newsrooms, are walking off the job for 24 hours beginning Wednesday and asking the public to honor a digital picket line. The roughly 150 members of the ProPublica Guild are in the midst of negotiating a collective bargaining agreement after unionizing in 2023. The union […]

articles.title: A new Anthropic model found security problems ‘in every major operating system and web browser’
articles.summary: Anthropic is debuting a new AI model as part of a cybersecurity partnership with Nvidia, Google, Amazon Web Services, Apple, Microsoft, and other companies. Project Glasswing, as it's called, is billed as a way for large companies, and potentially even the government, to flag vulnerabilities in their systems with virtually no human intervention. Anthropic is […]

articles.title: Suno and major music labels reportedly clash over AI music sharing
articles.summary: The AI-powered musicmaker Suno is struggling to reach licensing deals with Universal Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment. That's according to a report from the Financial Times, which says both sides can't agree on whether users should be able to share the AI-generated songs they create. "Universal wants AI-generated tracks to stay inside apps such […]

articles.title: Intel will help build Elon Musk’s Terafab AI chip factory
articles.summary: Elon Musk's Terafab AI chip project in Austin, Texas, is gaining a crucial new partner: Intel. On Tuesday, the American chipmaker announced it was signing on to help design and build the sprawling facility, which would supply AI chips to Musk's two companies, SpaceX (newly merged with xAI) and Tesla. Musk needs AI chips to […]

articles.title: Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
articles.summary: Google says it has updated Gemini to better direct users to get mental health resources during moments of crisis. The change comes as the tech giant faces a wrongful death lawsuit alleging its chatbot "coached" a man to die by suicide, the latest in a string of lawsuits alleging tangible harm from AI products. When […]
